Module com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java
Package com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java.extension

Interface ExtensibleArrayVerifier<S,​A,​E>

Type Parameters:
S - the type of verifier returned by the methods
A - the type of the array
E - the type of elements in the array
All Superinterfaces:
ExtensibleObjectVerifier<S,​A>
All Known Subinterfaces:
ArrayVerifier<A,​E>
All Known Implementing Classes:
AbstractArrayVerifier, AbstractArrayVerifierNoOp, ArrayVerifierImpl, ArrayVerifierNoOp
public interface ExtensibleArrayVerifier<S,​A,​E>
extends ExtensibleObjectVerifier<S,​A>
Verifies the requirements of an array of elements but the implementing verifier is not guaranteed to be a ArrayVerifier.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type Method Description
    CollectionVerifier<Collection<E>,​E> asCollection()
    Returns a verifier for the actual value as a collection.
    S asCollection​(Consumer<CollectionVerifier<Collection<E>,​E>> consumer)
    Verifies nested requirements.
    S contains​(E expected)
    Ensures that the actual value contains an element.
    S contains​(E expected, String name)
    Ensures that the actual value contains an element.
    S containsAll​(Collection<E> expected)
    Ensures that the actual value contains all of the specified elements.
    S containsAll​(Collection<E> expected, String name)
    Ensures that the actual value contains all of the specified elements.
    S containsAny​(Collection<E> expected)
    Ensures that the actual value contains any of the specified elements.
    S containsAny​(Collection<E> expected, String name)
    Ensures that the actual value contains any of the specified elements.
    S containsExactly​(Collection<E> expected)
    Ensures that the actual value contains the specified elements; nothing less, nothing more.
    S containsExactly​(Collection<E> expected, String name)
    Ensures that the actual value contains the specified elements; nothing less, nothing more.
    S doesNotContain​(E element)
    Ensures that the actual value does not contain an element.
    S doesNotContain​(E element, String name)
    Ensures that the actual value does not contain an element.
    S doesNotContainAll​(Collection<E> elements)
    Ensures that the actual value does not contain all of the specified elements.
    S doesNotContainAll​(Collection<E> elements, String name)
    Ensures that the actual value does not contain all of specified elements.
    S doesNotContainAny​(Collection<E> elements)
    Ensures that the actual value does not contain any of the specified elements.
    S doesNotContainAny​(Collection<E> elements, String name)
    Ensures that the actual value does not contain any of the specified elements.
    S doesNotContainDuplicates()
    Ensures that the actual value does not contain any duplicate elements.
    S doesNotContainExactly​(Collection<E> other)
    Ensures that the actual value does not contain exactly the specified elements; nothing less, nothing more.
    S doesNotContainExactly​(Collection<E> other, String name)
    Ensures that the actual value does not contain exactly the specified elements; nothing less, nothing more.
    S isEmpty()
    Ensures that the actual value is empty.
    S isNotEmpty()
    Ensures that the actual value is not empty.
    SizeVerifier length()
    Returns a verifier over the array's length.
    S length​(Consumer<SizeVerifier> consumer)
    Verifies nested requirements.

    Methods declared in interface com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java.extension.ExtensibleObjectVerifier

    asString, asString, getActual, isActualAvailable, isEqualTo, isEqualTo, isInstanceOf, isNotEqualTo, isNotEqualTo, isNotInstanceOf, isNotNull, isNotOneOf, isNotSameObjectAs, isNull, isOneOf, isSameObjectAs

  • Method Details

    • isEmpty

      S isEmpty()
      Ensures that the actual value is empty.
      Returns:
      the updated verifier
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the array is not empty

    • isNotEmpty

      S isNotEmpty()
      Ensures that the actual value is not empty.
      Returns:
      the updated verifier
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the array is empty

    • contains

      S contains​(E expected)
      Ensures that the actual value contains an element.
      Parameters:
      expected - the element
      Returns:
      the updated verifier
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the array does not contain expected

    • contains

      S contains​(E expected, String name)
      Ensures that the actual value contains an element.
      Parameters:
      expected - the element
      name - the name of the element
      Returns:
      the updated verifier
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if name is null
      IllegalArgumentException - if the array does not contain expected. If name is empty.

    • containsExactly

      S containsExactly​(Collection<E> expected)
      Ensures that the actual value contains the specified elements; nothing less, nothing more.
      Parameters:
      expected - the elements that must exist
      Returns:
      the updated verifier
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if expected is null
      IllegalArgumentException - if the array is missing any element found in expected; if the array contains any element not found in expected

    • containsExactly

      S containsExactly​(Collection<E> expected, String name)
      Ensures that the actual value contains the specified elements; nothing less, nothing more.
      Parameters:
      expected - the elements that must exist
      name - the name of the elements
      Returns:
      the updated verifier
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if expected or name are null
      IllegalArgumentException - if the array is missing any element found in expected. If the array contains any element not found in expected. If name is empty.

    • containsAny

      S containsAny​(Collection<E> expected)
      Ensures that the actual value contains any of the specified elements.
      Parameters:
      expected - the elements that must exist
      Returns:
      the updated verifier
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if expected is null
      IllegalArgumentException - if the array does not contain any of expected

    • containsAny

      S containsAny​(Collection<E> expected, String name)
      Ensures that the actual value contains any of the specified elements.
      Parameters:
      expected - the elements that must exist
      name - the name of the elements
      Returns:
      the updated verifier
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if expected or name are null
      IllegalArgumentException - if the array does not contain any of expected. If name is empty.

    • containsAll

      S containsAll​(Collection<E> expected)
      Ensures that the actual value contains all of the specified elements.
      Parameters:
      expected - the elements that must exist
      Returns:
      the updated verifier
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if expected is null
      IllegalArgumentException - if the array does not contain all of expected

    • containsAll

      S containsAll​(Collection<E> expected, String name)
      Ensures that the actual value contains all of the specified elements.
      Parameters:
      expected - the elements that must exist
      name - the name of the elements
      Returns:
      the updated verifier
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if expected or name are null
      IllegalArgumentException - if the array does not contain all of expected. If name is empty.

    • doesNotContain

      S doesNotContain​(E element)
      Ensures that the actual value does not contain an element.
      Parameters:
      element - the element that must not exist
      Returns:
      the updated verifier
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the array contains element

    • doesNotContain

      S doesNotContain​(E element, String name)
      Ensures that the actual value does not contain an element.
      Parameters:
      element - the element that must not exist
      name - the name of the element
      Returns:
      the updated verifier
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if name is null
      IllegalArgumentException - if the array contains element. If name is empty.

    • doesNotContainExactly

      S doesNotContainExactly​(Collection<E> other)
      Ensures that the actual value does not contain exactly the specified elements; nothing less, nothing more.
      Parameters:
      other - the elements that must not exist
      Returns:
      the updated verifier
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if other is null
      IllegalArgumentException - if the array contains all of the elements in other; nothing less, nothing more.

    • doesNotContainExactly

      S doesNotContainExactly​(Collection<E> other, String name)
      Ensures that the actual value does not contain exactly the specified elements; nothing less, nothing more.
      Parameters:
      other - the elements that must not exist
      name - the name of the collection
      Returns:
      the updated verifier
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if other or name are null
      IllegalArgumentException - if the array contains all of the elements in other; nothing less, nothing more. If name is empty.

    • doesNotContainAny

      S doesNotContainAny​(Collection<E> elements)
      Ensures that the actual value does not contain any of the specified elements.
      Parameters:
      elements - the elements that must not exist
      Returns:
      the updated verifier
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if elements is null
      IllegalArgumentException - if the array contains any of elements

    • doesNotContainAny

      S doesNotContainAny​(Collection<E> elements, String name)
      Ensures that the actual value does not contain any of the specified elements.
      Parameters:
      elements - the elements that must not exist
      name - the name of the elements
      Returns:
      the updated verifier
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if elements or name are null
      IllegalArgumentException - if the array contains any of elements. If name is empty.

    • doesNotContainAll

      S doesNotContainAll​(Collection<E> elements)
      Ensures that the actual value does not contain all of the specified elements.
      Parameters:
      elements - the elements that must not exist
      Returns:
      the updated verifier
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if elements is null
      IllegalArgumentException - if the array contains all of elements

    • doesNotContainAll

      S doesNotContainAll​(Collection<E> elements, String name)
      Ensures that the actual value does not contain all of specified elements.
      Parameters:
      elements - the elements that must not exist
      name - the name of the elements
      Returns:
      the updated verifier
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if elements or name are null
      IllegalArgumentException - if the array contains all of elements. If name is empty.

    • doesNotContainDuplicates

      S doesNotContainDuplicates()
      Ensures that the actual value does not contain any duplicate elements.
      Returns:
      the updated verifier
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the array contains any duplicate elements

    • length

      SizeVerifier length()
      Returns a verifier over the array's length.
      Returns:
      a verifier over the array's length

    • length

      S length​(Consumer<SizeVerifier> consumer)
      Verifies nested requirements. This mechanism can be used to group related requirements.
      Parameters:
      consumer - verifies the array's length
      Returns:
      the updated verifier
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if consumer is null

    • asCollection

      CollectionVerifier<Collection<E>,​E> asCollection()
      Returns a verifier for the actual value as a collection.
      Returns:
      a verifier for the actual value as a collection

    • asCollection

      S asCollection​(Consumer<CollectionVerifier<Collection<E>,​E>> consumer)
      Verifies nested requirements. This mechanism can be used to group related requirements.
      Parameters:
      consumer - verifies the actual value as a collection
      Returns:
      the updated verifier
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if consumer is null
